name: Mobilebusinessloan.com
description_short: Mobilebusinessloan.com provides business loans and personal lending services from an Atlanta location, with strong customer reviews highlighting fast approval and personalized service.
description_long:
Mobilebusinessloan.com operates as a loan agency based in Atlanta, Georgia at 1954 Airport Rd STE 130-67. The company is listed under multiple financial service categories including loan agency, financial consultant, and credit counseling service, though the primary focus appears to be business lending. The business maintains extended hours of operation, open Monday through Saturday from 12:00 AM to 11:59 PM, suggesting availability for customer inquiries and transactions throughout the day.

The company offers a range of lending products including business loans, personal loans, installment loans, and signature loans. They also provide broader financial services and credit counseling. Based on customer reviews, the company appears to work with an individual named McKenley or Mac who serves as a primary point of contact for business funding and investor connections. The service model emphasizes personalized relationships, with customers noting specific staff members by name in testimonials.

Mobilebusinessloan.com distinguishes itself through consistently high customer ratings (5.0 stars across available reviews) and testimonials emphasizing speed of service, professional conduct, and competitive rates. Long-term business relationships are highlighted, with one customer noting nearly a decade of partnership history. The company appears to market itself as a connector between businesses seeking funding and investors, not solely as a direct lender. Customer reviews repeatedly mention exceeding promised timelines and delivering professional-grade service.

However, there are significant caveats: the company listing shows miscellaneous categories (battery wholesaler, green energy supplier) that don't align with core services, suggesting either outdated or inaccurate business classification. No specific information is available regarding APR rates, loan terms, minimum/maximum amounts, approval criteria, or licensing status. Customer reviews, while positive, are limited in number (5 total) and all appear highly favorable without critical feedback. There is no verifiable independent regulatory or licensing information provided on the cited pages.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: Perfect 5.0-star rating across all available customer reviews
Fast loan processing—testimonials specifically mention exceeding promised timelines
Personalized service with named staff contacts (McKenley/Mac) maintaining long-term relationships
Competitive rates explicitly mentioned by satisfied customers
Extended hours of operation (open Monday-Saturday, 12 AM–11:59 PM)
Multi-decade partnership history with some business clients (one customer reports 10 years)
Services include business loans, personal loans, installment loans, and signature loans
cons: No information disclosed regarding APR rates, loan terms, or fee structures
Limited online transparency—no details on minimum/maximum loan amounts or approval criteria
Business listing includes unrelated categories (battery wholesaler, green energy supplier) suggesting inaccurate or outdated classification
Only 5 total customer reviews available; no independent third-party ratings from major financial review platforms
No verifiable information regarding company licensing, regulatory oversight, or compliance certifications
best_for: Small business owners seeking personalized loan relationships and competitive rates
Entrepreneurs looking to connect with investors through a financial intermediary
Borrowers who value long-term business relationships over transactional lending
Business owners in the Atlanta area who can meet in person for consultations
diagnosis: Mobilebusinessloan.com appears designed for small business owners and entrepreneurs seeking personalized, relationship-based lending with faster approval than traditional banks. The primary caveat is the complete lack of transparency regarding rates, terms, licensing, and regulatory status—borrowers should independently verify credentials and obtain detailed loan documentation before proceeding. The limited review sample and absence of third-party verification sources require caution.
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
